title: grevillea integrifolia subsp. ceratocarpa
intro: Grevillea integrifolia subsp. ceratocarpa, also known as the Western Silky Oak, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae, native to Australia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Grevillea integrifolia subsp. ceratocarpa are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
